# Tentacular Documentation
> Agentic-friendly platform for secure, durable, autonomic workflows on Kubernetes

## About
Tentacular provides "muscle memory" for AI agents — token-efficient, hardened, reusable
business logic deployed as TypeScript DAGs on Kubernetes. Workflows (called "tentacles")
are authored by AI agents, secured by a contract-driven zero-trust model, and managed
through an in-cluster MCP server.

## Repositories
- CLI + Engine: https://github.com/randybias/tentacular
- MCP Server: https://github.com/randybias/tentacular-mcp
- Agent Skill: https://github.com/randybias/tentacular-skill
- Template Catalog: https://github.com/randybias/tentacular-catalog

## Key Concepts
- Tentacle: A deployed workflow — a DAG of TypeScript nodes with a contract, triggers, and config
- Contract: Security policy declaring external dependencies, driving NetworkPolicy, Deno permissions, and secrets validation
- Exoskeleton: Optional backing services (Postgres, NATS, RustFS) with per-tentacle isolation
- MCP Server: In-cluster control plane accessed via Model Context Protocol
- Agent Skill: Instruction set enabling AI agents to build, test, and deploy tentacles
